name: Brad Smith
title: Vice Chair and President
other_orgs: Board member of Netflix, Board of Trustees at Princeton University, Former General Counsel of Microsoft (2002-2015)
notes: Brad Smith is Vice Chair and President of Microsoft, where he oversees the company's legal, government affairs, and public policy functions. He has testified before Congress multiple times on AI policy, including before the Senate Commerce Committee in May 2025, where he advocated for federal AI infrastructure investment and workforce development. His 2019 book 'Tools and Weapons: The Promise and the Peril of the Digital Age' argued that governments must regulate technology by moving faster to match the pace of innovation. Smith has been a leading industry voice for responsible AI development, supporting Microsoft's Responsible AI principles while advocating for targeted regulation of high-risk AI applications.
belief_regulatory_stance_detail: Advocates for mandatory safety evaluations and transparency requirements for AI systems. Testified before Senate Commerce Committee in May 2025 supporting federal investment in AI infrastructure and workforce. His book 'Tools and Weapons' argued governments need to regulate technology by matching the pace of innovation. Supports targeted regulation of high-risk AI applications through existing and new federal frameworks.

name: Marc Benioff
title: Chair, CEO and Co-Founder
other_orgs: Co-owner of TIME magazine (acquired 2018), Former VP at Oracle Corporation (1986-1999), World Economic Forum board member
notes: Marc Benioff is Chair, CEO, and Co-Founder of Salesforce, a Fortune 150 cloud computing company he founded in 1999. Under his leadership, Salesforce has invested heavily in enterprise AI through its Einstein AI platform and the Agentforce product for deploying autonomous AI agents, which has been adopted by over 12,000 enterprise customers. Benioff has advocated for a stakeholder capitalism approach to technology, and Salesforce was an early adopter of responsible AI guidelines for its products. As co-owner of TIME magazine, he has also shaped public discourse on AI through editorial decisions including the TIME100 AI list.
belief_regulatory_stance_detail: Supports responsible AI development with corporate accountability. Salesforce has published AI Acceptable Use Policies and ethical AI guidelines. Has advocated for transparency in AI systems and responsible deployment through enterprise guardrails. In 2025 interviews, stated he does not foresee a white-collar jobs apocalypse from AI but emphasizes the need for workforce adaptation.

name: Ron DeSantis
title: Governor of Florida
other_orgs: Former U.S. Representative for Florida's 6th congressional district (2013-2018)
notes: Ron DeSantis is the Governor of Florida who has emerged as a prominent state-level voice on AI regulation. In December 2025, he proposed a 'Citizen Bill of Rights for Artificial Intelligence' covering data privacy, parental controls, consumer protections, and restrictions on AI use of individuals' likenesses without consent. The proposal also includes bans on state use of Chinese AI tools like DeepSeek and protections for ratepayers from data center energy costs. DeSantis has publicly defied President Trump's executive order seeking to preempt state AI regulation, arguing that states retain the right to regulate AI and that executive orders cannot override state authority.
belief_regulatory_stance_detail: Proposed Florida AI Bill of Rights (December 2025) focused on consumer protection, data privacy, and restrictions on AI-generated deepfakes. Opposes both federal preemption of state AI regulation and unrestricted AI deployment. Supports banning Chinese AI tools in government and protecting ratepayers from data center energy costs. Argues states have the constitutional right to regulate AI regardless of federal executive orders.
